data.content:
# Claude Office Skills - Context Documentation This repository provides programmatic Office document manipulation capabilities for PowerPoint (PPTX), Word (DOCX), Excel (XLSX), and PDF files. Originally designed for Claude Code CLI workflows, these skills enable automated document creation, template-based generation, text replacement, form filling, and comprehensive editing with tracked changes. All operations preserve formatting, use working formulas rather than hardcoded values, and follow professional document standards with zero-error validation requirements. The system follows a skills-based architecture where each document format has dedicated workflows documented in SKILL.md files. Python and JavaScript utilities handle the heavy lifting: unpacking OOXML archives for direct XML manipulation, extracting structured inventories of text content, applying batch replacements with formatting preservation, converting HTML designs to PowerPoint slides, and managing tracked changes in Word documents. All generated files organize into `outputs//` directories with intermediate artifacts (unpacked XML, JSON inventories, thumbnail grids) kept alongside final outputs for debugging and iteration. --- ## PowerPoint: Extract Text Inventory Extract all text shapes from a presentation with positions, formatting, and placeholder types. Returns JSON with slide/shape hierarchy preserving paragraph-level formatting (bullets, alignment, fonts, colors, spacing). ```bash # Extract structured text inventory to JSON venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/inventory.py presentation.pptx outputs/analysis/inventory.json # Read the generated JSON to see all text content cat outputs/analysis/inventory.json ``` ```json { "slide-0": { "shape-0": { "placeholder_type": "TITLE", "left": 0.5, "top": 0.5, "width": 9.0, "height": 1.2, "default_font_size": 44.0, "paragraphs": [ { "text": "Quarterly Sales Report", "alignment": "CENTER", "bold": true, "font_size": 44.0, "font_name": "Arial" } ] }, "shape-1": { "placeholder_type": "BODY", "left": 0.5, "top": 2.0, "width": 9.0, "height": 3.0, "paragraphs": [ { "text": "Revenue increased 15% year-over-year", "bullet": true, "level": 0 }, { "text": "New customers up 22%", "bullet": true, "level": 0 } ] } } } ``` ## PowerPoint: Replace Text from JSON Apply text replacements to shapes using JSON with formatting. All shapes from inventory are cleared unless specified in replacements. Validates shape existence and preserves template design. ```bash # Create replacement JSON with new content and formatting cat > outputs/sales-deck/replacements.json << 'EOF' { "slide-0": { "shape-0": { "paragraphs": [ {"text": "Q4 2024 Results", "alignment": "CENTER", "bold": true} ] }, "shape-1": { "paragraphs": [ {"text": "Revenue: $2.4M (+18%)", "bullet": true, "level": 0}, {"text": "Customers: 1,250 (+25%)", "bullet": true, "level": 0}, {"text": "Retention: 94%", "bullet": true, "level": 0, "color": "70AD47"} ] } } } EOF # Apply replacements to presentation venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/replace.py \ outputs/sales-deck/working.pptx \ outputs/sales-deck/replacements.json \ outputs/sales-deck/final.pptx # Validate by creating thumbnail grid venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/thumbnail.py \ outputs/sales-deck/final.pptx \ outputs/sales-deck/review ``` ## PowerPoint: Rearrange Slides Duplicate, reorder, and delete slides by specifying a sequence of 0-based indices. Repeating indices duplicates slides, omitted indices are deleted. ```bash # Create new presentation using slides 0, 5 (twice), 12, and 8 from template # Original slide 5 gets duplicated for two different content sections venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/rearrange.py \ template.pptx \ outputs/custom-deck/rearranged.pptx \ 0,5,5,12,8 # Verify the structure with thumbnail grid venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/thumbnail.py \ outputs/custom-deck/rearranged.pptx \ outputs/custom-deck/thumbnails --cols 4 ``` ## PowerPoint: Create Thumbnail Grid Generate visual thumbnail grids (JPEG) for quick slide review. Automatically splits large decks into multiple grids, labels slides with 0-based indices. ```bash # Basic thumbnail grid with default settings (5 columns, 30 slides/grid) venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/thumbnail.py \ presentation.pptx \ outputs/review/grid # Custom layout with 4 columns (20 slides per grid) venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/thumbnail.py \ template.pptx \ outputs/review/template --cols 4 ``` Creates `outputs/review/grid.jpg` or `grid-1.jpg`, `grid-2.jpg` for large decks. ## PowerPoint: HTML to PPTX Conversion Convert HTML slides to PowerPoint with pixel-perfect positioning using html2pptx.js library. Supports text formatting, shapes, images, and placeholder regions for charts. ```javascript // Generate HTML slides (720pt × 405pt for 16:9) const fs = require('fs'); const html = `

Q4 Performance

$2.4M Revenue

`; fs.writeFileSync('outputs/report/slide1.html', html); // Convert HTML to PowerPoint and add chart const pptxgen = require('pptxgenjs'); const html2pptx = require('./public/pptx/scripts/html2pptx.js'); const pptx = new pptxgen(); pptx.layout = 'LAYOUT_16x9'; const { slide, placeholders } = await html2pptx('outputs/report/slide1.html', pptx); // Add chart to placeholder area if (placeholders.length > 0) { slide.addChart(pptx.charts.BAR, [{ name: "Revenue", labels: ["Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4"], values: [1.8, 2.1, 2.2, 2.4] }], { ...placeholders[0], showTitle: true, title: 'Quarterly Revenue ($M)', chartColors: ["3498db"] }); } await pptx.writeFile('outputs/report/presentation.pptx'); ``` ## PowerPoint: Unpack and Edit OOXML Unpack PPTX to raw XML files for direct editing of speaker notes, comments, animations, and complex formatting. Validate and repack after changes. ```bash # Unpack presentation to XML files venv/bin/python public/pptx/ooxml/scripts/unpack.py \ presentation.pptx \ outputs/editing/unpacked/ # Edit XML files (e.g., add speaker notes to slide 1) # File: outputs/editing/unpacked/ppt/notesSlides/notesSlide1.xml # Validate changes against original venv/bin/python public/pptx/ooxml/scripts/validate.py \ outputs/editing/unpacked/ \ --original presentation.pptx # Repack to PPTX venv/bin/python public/pptx/ooxml/scripts/pack.py \ outputs/editing/unpacked/ \ outputs/editing/modified.pptx ``` ## PowerPoint: Extract Text with Markdown Convert presentation content to markdown for text analysis and content review. Preserves slide structure and formatting. ```bash # Extract all text to markdown venv/bin/python -m markitdown presentation.pptx > outputs/analysis/content.md # Read extracted content cat outputs/analysis/content.md ``` ## Word: Add Tracked Changes (Redlining) Implement professional document review with tracked changes. Uses Document library for precise XML manipulation while preserving RSIDs and change metadata. ```python # File: outputs/contract-review/add_changes.py import sys sys.path.insert(0, 'public/docx/scripts') from document import Document doc = Document('outputs/contract-review/unpacked') # Find paragraph with specific text para_node = doc.get_node('//w:p[contains(., "30 days")]') # Add tracked deletion and insertion (minimal edit principle) # Only mark the actual change: "30" → "60" runs = para_node.findall('.//w:r', doc.ns) for run in runs: text_elem = run.find('.//w:t', doc.ns) if text_elem is not None and '30 days' in text_elem.text: # Extract RSID from original run rsid = run.get(f'{{{doc.ns["w"]}}}rsidR', '00AB12CD') # Create: [unchanged] + [deletion] + [insertion] + [unchanged] unchanged_before = doc.create_run('The term is ', rsid=rsid) deletion = doc.create_tracked_delete('30') insertion = doc.create_tracked_insert('60') unchanged_after = doc.create_run(' days.', rsid=rsid) # Replace run para_node.insert(para_node.index(run), unchanged_before) para_node.insert(para_node.index(run), deletion) para_node.insert(para_node.index(run), insertion) para_node.insert(para_node.index(run), unchanged_after) para_node.remove(run) break doc.save() ``` ```bash # Workflow: Unpack, edit, pack, verify venv/bin/python public/docx/ooxml/scripts/unpack.py contract.docx outputs/contract-review/unpacked/ venv/bin/python outputs/contract-review/add_changes.py venv/bin/python public/docx/ooxml/scripts/pack.py outputs/contract-review/unpacked/ outputs/contract-review/reviewed.docx # Verify tracked changes appear correctly pandoc --track-changes=all outputs/contract-review/reviewed.docx -o outputs/contract-review/verification.md grep "60 days" outputs/contract-review/verification.md ``` ## Word: Extract Text with Tracked Changes Convert Word documents to markdown with tracked changes preserved. Useful for content review and verification. ```bash # Extract with all tracked changes visible pandoc --track-changes=all document.docx -o outputs/analysis/content.md # Extract with changes accepted pandoc --track-changes=accept document.docx -o outputs/analysis/clean.md # Extract with changes rejected pandoc --track-changes=reject document.docx -o outputs/analysis/original.md ``` ## Excel: Create Financial Model with Formulas Build spreadsheets with working formulas, color-coded inputs/calculations, and zero-error validation. Always use formulas, never hardcoded calculations. ```python from openpyxl import Workbook from openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill, Alignment wb = Workbook() ws = wb.active ws.title = "Revenue Model" # Headers ws['A1'] = 'Year' ws['B1'] = 'Revenue ($M)' ws['C1'] = 'Growth %' ws['A1'].font = Font(bold=True) ws['B1'].font = Font(bold=True) ws['C1'].font = Font(bold=True) # Color-coded inputs (blue text) blue_font = Font(color='0000FF') # Blue for inputs ws['A2'], ws['B2'] = '2024', 10.5 ws['A2'].font = blue_font ws['B2'].font = blue_font ws['A2'].number_format = '@' # Format year as text # Growth assumption (blue, yellow highlight) ws['C2'] = 0.15 ws['C2'].font = blue_font ws['C2'].fill = PatternFill(start_color='FFFF00', fill_type='solid') ws['C2'].number_format = '0.0%' # Formulas (black text, auto-default) ws['A3'] = '=TEXT(VALUE(A2)+1,"0")' # Next year as text ws['B3'] = '=B2*(1+$C$2)' # Revenue with growth ws['C3'] = '=(B3-B2)/B2' # Calculate actual growth ws['A3'].number_format = '@' ws['B3'].number_format = '$#,##0.0;($#,##0.0);"-"' # Show zeros as "-" ws['C3'].number_format = '0.0%' # Copy formula down for row in range(4, 8): ws[f'A{row}'] = f'=TEXT(VALUE(A{row-1})+1,"0")' ws[f'B{row}'] = f'=B{row-1}*(1+$C$2)' ws[f'C{row}'] = f'=(B{row}-B{row-1})/B{row-1}' ws[f'A{row}'].number_format = '@' ws[f'B{row}'].number_format = '$#,##0.0;($#,##0.0);"-"' ws[f'C{row}'].number_format = '0.0%' wb.save('outputs/budget/revenue_model.xlsx') ``` ```bash # Recalculate formulas and check for errors venv/bin/python public/xlsx/scripts/recalc.py outputs/budget/revenue_model.xlsx # Output validates zero errors: # {"status": "success", "total_errors": 0, "total_formulas": 18} ``` ## Excel: Edit Existing Spreadsheet Load, modify, and save Excel files while preserving formulas and formatting. Use cell references for dynamic calculations. ```python from openpyxl import load_workbook # Load existing file wb = load_workbook('template.xlsx') ws = wb['Summary'] # Update input cell (recalculates dependent formulas) ws['B5'] = 125000 # Update revenue assumption # Add new calculated row with formula ws['A10'] = 'Profit Margin' ws['B10'] = '=B8/B5' # Formula referencing existing cells ws['B10'].number_format = '0.0%' # Insert row and shift down ws.insert_rows(6) ws['A6'] = 'New Expense' ws['B6'] = '=B5*0.1' # 10% of revenue wb.save('outputs/budget/updated.xlsx') ``` ```bash # Recalculate all formulas venv/bin/python public/xlsx/scripts/recalc.py outputs/budget/updated.xlsx ``` ## PDF: Fill Form Fields Programmatically populate fillable PDF forms with validation. Checks field existence, types, and page numbers before writing. ```python # File: outputs/application/fill_form.py import json from pypdf import PdfReader, PdfWriter from public.pdf.scripts.extract_form_field_info import get_field_info # Create field values JSON fields = [ {"field_id": "applicant_name", "page": 1, "value": "John Smith"}, {"field_id": "email", "page": 1, "value": "john.smith@example.com"}, {"field_id": "start_date", "page": 1, "value": "2024-01-15"}, {"field_id": "department", "page": 2, "value": "Engineering"} ] with open('outputs/application/fields.json', 'w') as f: json.dump(fields, f, indent=2) ``` ```bash # List available form fields venv/bin/python public/pdf/scripts/extract_form_field_info.py form.pdf # Fill form with validation venv/bin/python public/pdf/scripts/fill_fillable_fields.py \ form.pdf \ outputs/application/fields.json \ outputs/application/completed.pdf # Create visual validation (highlights filled fields) venv/bin/python public/pdf/scripts/create_validation_image.py \ outputs/application/completed.pdf \ outputs/application/validation.png ``` ## PDF: Merge Multiple Documents Combine multiple PDF files into a single document with page ordering control. ```python from pypdf import PdfWriter, PdfReader writer = PdfWriter() # Merge complete documents for pdf_file in ['cover.pdf', 'report.pdf', 'appendix.pdf']: reader = PdfReader(pdf_file) for page in reader.pages: writer.add_page(page) # Add specific pages from another document reader = PdfReader('data.pdf') writer.add_page(reader.pages[0]) # Only first page writer.add_page(reader.pages[5]) # Only sixth page with open('outputs/final-report/merged.pdf', 'wb') as f: writer.write(f) ``` ## PDF: Extract Tables to Excel Extract structured table data from PDFs and export to Excel for analysis. ```python import pdfplumber import pandas as pd all_tables = [] with pdfplumber.open('report.pdf') as pdf: for i, page in enumerate(pdf.pages): tables = page.extract_tables() for j, table in enumerate(tables): if table and len(table) > 1: # First row as headers df = pd.DataFrame(table[1:], columns=table[0]) df['source_page'] = i + 1 df['table_number'] = j + 1 all_tables.append(df) if all_tables: combined = pd.concat(all_tables, ignore_index=True) combined.to_excel('outputs/data-extraction/tables.xlsx', index=False) print(f"Extracted {len(all_tables)} tables to Excel") ``` ## PDF: Convert PowerPoint to PDF Convert presentations to PDF for distribution or visual analysis using LibreOffice. ```bash # Convert PPTX to PDF soffice --headless --convert-to pdf --outdir outputs/presentation/ presentation.pptx # Convert PDF pages to images for inspection pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 outputs/presentation/presentation.pdf outputs/presentation/page # Creates: page-1.jpg, page-2.jpg, ... ``` --- ## Main Use Cases and Integration Patterns This repository excels at automated document generation workflows where templates need population with dynamic data, presentations require assembly from slide libraries, or documents need batch processing with tracked changes. The template-based PowerPoint workflow (rearrange → inventory → replace) enables creating customized decks from master templates without design work—extract text inventory, map content to appropriate slide layouts, generate replacement JSON with formatting, and apply changes while preserving the template's visual design. Similarly, the Word redlining workflow supports systematic document review at scale: convert to markdown for analysis, identify changes in batches, implement tracked edits with minimal RSID-preserving replacements, and verify with pandoc conversion. Integration patterns center on the `outputs//` convention for managing document projects with all artifacts in one place. HTML-to-PPTX workflows create slide designs in HTML/CSS, rasterize gradients and icons to PNG with Sharp, render through Playwright, and add charts with PptxGenJS—keeping intermediate HTML files, images, and thumbnails organized for iteration. Excel workflows emphasize formula-based models over hardcoded calculations, using color-coded inputs (blue) and formulas (black) with mandatory `recalc.py` validation for zero-error delivery. PDF form filling validates field existence and types before writing, generating visual overlays to confirm correct population. All workflows support CI/CD integration: scripts run headless, output JSON for parsing, and organize files predictably for artifact collection and quality gates.
